channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Justice Department to allow firing squads for executions in move to ramp up capital punishment
item.description: The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department will adopt firing squad as a permitted method of execution as the Trump administration moves to ramp up and expedite capital punishment cases.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Workers in Arizona don't have protections they need from triple-digit temperatures
item.description: Labor groups say Arizona isn't moving fast enough to establish new protections for people whose work exposes them to the state's sizzling high temperatures.
item.content:encoded: Labor groups say Arizona isn't moving fast enough to establish new protections for people whose work exposes them to the state's sizzling high temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Davis-Young

item.title: Appeals court rules that Trump's asylum ban at the border is illegal
item.description: A U.S. appeals court ruled Friday that immigration laws allow people to apply for asylum at the border, and the president cannot bypass this. The decision stems from Trump declaring the border situation an invasion and suspending asylum.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. appeals court ruled Friday that immigration laws allow people to apply for asylum at the border, and the president cannot bypass this. The decision stems from Trump declaring the border situation an invasion and suspending asylum.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Justice Department drops probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: The move paves the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, the president's nominee to head the central bank.
item.content:encoded:
The move paves the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, the president's nominee to head the central bank.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk

item.title: Decades-old, newly restored Smithsonian carousel reopens — to children's delight
item.description: The carousel was first desegregated when part of Gwynn Oak Amusement Park outside Baltimore in 1963. It was moved to the National Mall after the park closed.
item.content:encoded:
The carousel was first desegregated when part of Gwynn Oak Amusement Park outside Baltimore in 1963. It was moved to the National Mall after the park closed.

(Image credit: Valerie Plesch for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: How Pittsburgh — host of this year's NFL draft — became a sports mecca
item.description: The Pennsylvania city is hosting the draft for the first time in almost 80 years. Pittsburghers say the city's passionate fanbases and winning teams make the selection a natural fit.
item.content:encoded:
The Pennsylvania city is hosting the draft for the first time in almost 80 years. Pittsburghers say the city's passionate fanbases and winning teams make the selection a natural fit.

(Image credit: Jeff Swensen for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: At a veteran-led protest on Capitol Hill, grief and dismay reverberate over Iran war
item.description: Photos and videos from the protest, which resulted in 66 arrests, have spread widely across social media — amplified by others who share a similar frustration and unease about the country's military action.
item.content:encoded:
Photos and videos from the protest, which resulted in 66 arrests, have spread widely across social media — amplified by others who share a similar frustration and unease about the country's military action.

(Image credit: Leigh Vogel)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: After 2 failed votes, Mike Johnson unveils new plan to extend key U.S. spy powers
item.description: With an April 30 deadline fast approaching, Johnson unveiled his latest proposal to extend the controversial surveillance program known as FISA 702.
item.content:encoded:
With an April 30 deadline fast approaching, Johnson unveiled his latest proposal to extend the controversial surveillance program known as FISA 702.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: DHS starts deportation of woman whose loved one died in the Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.
item.content:encoded:
The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.

(Image credit: Wesley Lapointe for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Congress wants to stop corporations from buying up too many single family homes
item.description: It is getting harder and harder to buy a home in the U.S. Congress believes one solution is to put restrictions on corporate ownership of single family homes. Will it really help?
item.content:encoded: It is getting harder and harder to buy a home in the U.S. Congress believes one solution is to put restrictions on corporate ownership of single family homes. Will it really help?
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods
